description: Command Injection occurs when untrusted input is passed to a system shell without proper sanitisation. An attacker can append OS commands to application-level calls, achieving arbitrary code execution under the web-server process account.

related.technique_name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell

mitre_attack.mapping_note: T1190 covers exploitation of the vulnerable endpoint; T1059.004 applies when the injected command is executed by a Unix shell.

cvss_methodology.note: CVSS measures vulnerability severity. It does not prescribe SOC response deadlines.
cvss_methodology.migration_note: CVSS v4.0 is current, but its metrics and scoring are not one-to-one with v3.1. Existing scenario vectors remain v3.1 until individually re-assessed.

response_framework.name: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3
response_framework.note: Response events implement Detect, Respond, Recover, and continuous-improvement outcomes. Numeric exercise thresholds are local policy, not NIST requirements.

timing_model.note: The 900-second floor prevents zero-length and division-by-zero windows at CVSS 10.0. These values are simulation policy and must be calibrated with organizational risk and service objectives.

incident_response_rules.description: Alert triage started within the difficulty-adjusted MTTA threshold
incident_response_rules.trigger: alert_investigation_started occurs after mtta_threshold_sec or is absent

incident_response_rules.description: Incident assessed and confirmed or formally dismissed
incident_response_rules.trigger: neither incident_confirmed nor dismissal_approved is present

incident_response_rules.description: Relevant evidence preserved before destructive containment
incident_response_rules.trigger: incident_confirmed is present but evidence_preserved is absent or occurs after containment_initiated
containment_initiated is absent or occurs after ttc_max_sec

incident_response_rules.description: Root cause or malicious artifact eradicated after containment
incident_response_rules.trigger: containment_succeeded is present but eradication_completed is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Recovery validated before normal operation resumed
incident_response_rules.trigger: eradication_completed is present but recovery_validated is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Lessons learned and improvement actions recorded
incident_response_rules.trigger: recovery_validated is present but lessons_learned_recorded is absent or out of order

incident_response_rules.description: Medium-or-higher alerts are not denied without documented approval
incident_response_rules.trigger: alert_denied is present for severity medium or higher without a preceding dismissal_approved event

scoring.ttc_factor: 1 when containment succeeds by ttc_expected; linear decay to 0 at ttc_max; 0 when containment is absent or later than ttc_max

response_difficulty_bonus.condition: awarded only when containment_succeeded is present
response_difficulty_bonus.zero_delay_policy: A zero-second investigation delay uses a one-second denominator and therefore receives the capped bonus.

stock_rules.description: Common web attack pattern, including selected command and traversal strings

custom_rules.description: Web request contains shell metacharacters and the serving process spawns a shell or utility
Application request is followed by abnormal sleep syscalls or DNS queries to a newly observed domain
Authenticated API request is correlated with an unexpected child process under the application service account

wazuh.note: Custom entries are dataset detection specifications and must be implemented and tested in the deployed Wazuh manager before use. Stock rule descriptions and levels match the linked upstream ruleset.

scenarios.label: Unauthenticated OS Command Injection via Web Form
scenarios.description: Attacker submits a crafted HTTP request to a ping-utility endpoint, injecting a semicolon-delimited shell command. No authentication required. Full command execution under www-data. Scope unchanged (single web process).

attack_context.rationale: Authentication requirement follows the endpoint access described by the scenario.

component_rationale.AC_L: No special conditions; form input is directly concatenated to shell call
component_rationale.S_U: Impact confined to web-server process boundary
component_rationale.C_H: Full read access to server filesystem and memory
component_rationale.A_H: Attacker can terminate processes or exhaust resources

detection.rationale: Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100201 (Shell metacharacters correlated with a spawned process); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Shell metacharacters correlated with a spawned process

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100201 level 9 fired: Shell metacharacters correlated with a spawned process.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 300s, within 750s medium threshold. Wazuh correlation shows semicolon in request body: 'host=127.0.0.1;id'
Analyst-2 replayed request in sandbox — confirmed RCE; output of 'id' command returned in HTTP response. Shell confirmed as www-data.
Relevant logs, request data, volatile evidence, and indicators were preserved with chain-of-custody metadata.
Analyst-3 deployed WAF rule blocking semicolons and backticks in ping endpoint parameter; source IP blocked at perimeter firewall.
Probe from attacker IP blocked; WAF returning 403. Application-level input validation patch queued for next deployment window.
Malicious artifacts and the vulnerable execution path were removed or disabled; root cause remediation was recorded.
Service health, security controls, and monitoring were validated before normal operation resumed.
Post-incident review captured root cause, response gaps, owners, and tracked improvement actions.

scenarios.label: Blind Time-Based Command Injection via Authenticated Endpoint (Scope Changed)
scenarios.description: Authenticated low-privilege user exploits a server-side report-generation endpoint via blind time-based injection. The injected payload exfiltrates data via out-of-band DNS queries. Scope changed because the pivot reaches a secondary backend service outside the web process.

attack_context.rationale: Attacker must hold a valid low-privilege user session to access the report-generation endpoint.

component_rationale.AC_H: Requires timing analysis to confirm injection; OOB channel setup needed
component_rationale.S_C: Attacker pivots from web process to backend DB service
component_rationale.C_H: Full read of backend database contents via OOB exfiltration
component_rationale.I_H: Write access to backend filesystem via injected commands
component_rationale.A_L: Limited availability impact; service degradation possible from timing payloads

detection.rationale: Blind injection has no direct response output; the OOB DNS exfiltration channel is subtle and requires correlation of DNS resolver logs with application audit trails. Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100202 (Timing or DNS behavior correlated with a request parameter); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Timing or DNS behavior correlated with a request parameter

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100202 level 8 fired: Timing or DNS behavior correlated with a request parameter.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 750s, within 900s high threshold. Initial review flagged alert as potentially false-positive due to legitimate DNS traffic patterns.
The analyst denied the alert without documented approval. This triggers CMDI-R09; all other missing response actions are evaluated independently.

scenarios.label: Authenticated Low-Privilege Command Injection with Delayed Containment
scenarios.description: Low-privilege authenticated user injects commands into a file-conversion utility. The injection is partially sanitised (limited I/A impact), scope unchanged. The SOC detects and contains within the expected window despite delayed investigation.

attack_context.rationale: Attacker requires a valid low-privilege session to reach the file-conversion endpoint.

component_rationale.AC_L: Standard injection; no special environment conditions
component_rationale.S_U: Impact confined to web-server process; no scope change
component_rationale.C_H: Full read of files accessible to web process (credentials, configs)
component_rationale.I_L: Limited write capability; partial file overwrite only
component_rationale.A_L: Limited availability impact; service slowdown possible

detection.rationale: auditd syscall events for execve() combined with web server logs provide clear injection evidence; standard Wazuh correlation rules apply. Detection uses custom Wazuh rule 100203 (Authenticated request correlated with an unexpected execve event); the rule definition and required telemetry are documented in this attack record.

wazuh_alert.description: Authenticated request correlated with an unexpected execve event

event_log.detail: Wazuh custom rule 100203 level 8 fired: Authenticated request correlated with an unexpected execve event.
Analyst-1 opened alert — MTTA 600s, within 750s medium threshold. Correlation of auditd execve log with web access log confirmed injection vector in filename parameter.
Analyst-2 confirmed injected command 'cat /etc/passwd' executed. Credential file read confirmed from process output captured in auditd.
Relevant logs, request data, volatile evidence, and indicators were preserved with chain-of-custody metadata.
Analyst-3 applied input sanitisation hotfix to filename parameter (allowlist of alphanumeric and dot); test_user account suspended pending investigation.
Re-test of injection vector returns 400 Bad Request. Hotfix validated. Credential rotation ticket raised. TTC_actual=8000s < TTC_expected=8640s.
Malicious artifacts and the vulnerable execution path were removed or disabled; root cause remediation was recorded.
Service health, security controls, and monitoring were validated before normal operation resumed.
Post-incident review captured root cause, response gaps, owners, and tracked improvement actions.

references.title: FIRST CVSS v4.0 Specification (current migration target)

dataset_metadata.source_policy: Use upstream standards and vendor rulesets; label project-specific detection logic as custom.
dataset_metadata.cvss_profile: Validated CVSS v3.1 compatibility profile; migrate through a separate expert review rather than automatic vector conversion.
